title: Foreman Cart
summary: Making a rolling Kreg foreman cart using the universal workbenches.
description: Making a rolling Kreg foreman cart using the universal workbenches. The storage is awesome to keep screws and other jigs ready to grab and use in seconds! Check the Youtube video for any questions! Video link is in Extras Tab.

steps.title: Assemble the Universal Workbench
steps.body: Begin assembly of the 20"x 44" Kreg Universal Workbench with standard height legs that can be adjusted from 31" to 39" tall. It can be built without the wheels and as a stationary unit, but I like being able to move my shop tools around if I need the extra workspace so I added the wheel kit.

steps.title: Base and Back
steps.body: Cut the base to fit the opening, the cabinets and Kreg Foreman will rest on the base. The Base will measure, roughly 20"x 43 7/8". While its all open cut a back panel for the inside of the Universal Bench. This will help keeping the unit square and rigid. Screw from the outside into the plywood. The workbench has hole openings for this purpose.

steps.title: Two side cabinets
steps.body: Cut 4 pieces measuring, 18 1/4" tall by 18" deep. These will be the sides for the tall cabinets. Cut 4 pieces that measure, 9 1/2" by 18" deep. These will be the tops and bottoms for the cabinets. Pocket hole the top and bottoms to the side cabinets. Cut a back panel to size, brad nail or screw on the back.

steps.title: Middle Cabinet
steps.body: Cut 2 sides measuring 10 3/8" tall by 18" deep. Cut the bottoms 16 1/2" wide by 18" deep. Pocket hole the cabinet together. Cut a back panel to size and attach the back.

steps.title: Platform for the Kreg Forman
steps.body: The Foreman will rest on a single piece of plywood measuring 18" wide by 27 1/2" deep.

steps.title: Top of Workbench
steps.body: There is two options, Cut a long piece and jigsaw out the opening for the Kreg Foreman after its been attached or cut two individual tops. I went with cutting two individual tops. However looking back maybe a jigsaw would have been much easier. I cut, two top pieces from a 3/4" plywood measuring 23"x16". I screwed from the cabinets below into the top. Once the tops are secure, I secured the tops through the holes on the Kreg Universal Workbench to keep everything secured together.

steps.title: Adjustable Shelf Pin Jig
steps.body: Now with all the cabinets assembled, I used the Kreg Adjustable Shelf Pin jig to make holes for either adjustable shelves or drawers. This is up to everyone and their shop organization goals. I like drawers.

steps.title: Insert Kreg Foreman
steps.body: Once the unit is assembled, I used a combination of a few bolts, nuts, and washers to get the keg foreman level with the tops.

steps.title: Peg Board
steps.body: This is optional, you can just attach a solid piece if you wanted. I ripped a couple spacers and attached them into the side cabinets to give the peg board something to attach to with a couple screws.
